finding_summary: constellation.md asserts (3 loci in node body + 1 in wave-207 sidecar) that Rosewater's 'At Death's Door, Part 2' (2020-01-06) previews Eutropia the Twice-Favored as the Constellation-on-non-enchantment demonstration card. WebFetch verification confirms the article's actual previewed Constellation card is Archon of Sun's Grace, not Eutropia. The design-knob claim (Journey into Nyx was enchantments-only; solution was extending to non-enchantments) DOES hold per WebFetch. Eutropia IS a non-enchantment Constellation creature in THB and IS therefore a design-knob beneficiary, but she is NOT the article's previewed card. Origin: card trivia at 216-eutropia-the-twice-favored.md hedged carefully ('Cards like Eutropia were described as the key knob') but the node-body wave-207 commission over-claimed to 'Eutropia is canonically the article's preview-example demonstration card'.

fixes.before: The cohort's most BBL-relevant property is its **direct dual-coding of Theros's cosmology as combat-rules**. Theros's central cosmological premise is that Nyx — the starry-night realm where the gods dwell — bleeds into the mortal plane whenever an enchantment is cast, because enchantments in Theros canon ARE Nyx-substance manifest on the mortal plane (per the *Theros Beyond Death Story Summary*, Wizards of the Coast 2020-01-10, and the Enchantment Creature / Nymph creature-type design from original *Theros* 2013). Constellation renders this *literal* at the rules-text level: every enchantment entering is a Nyx-event, every Constellation trigger is the mortal-plane reacting to that Nyx-event. The mechanic's narrative read is the gods themselves noticing — the constellation lights up, and the mortal plane's enchantment-coded creatures and spells respond.
fixes.after:
The cohort's most BBL-relevant property is its **direct dual-coding of Theros's cosmology as combat-rules**. Theros's central cosmological premise is that Nyx — the starry-night realm where the gods dwell — bleeds into the mortal plane whenever an enchantment is cast, because enchantments in Theros canon ARE Nyx-substance manifest on the mortal plane (per the *Theros Beyond Death Story Summary*, Wizards of the Coast 2020-01-10, and the Enchantment Creature / Nymph creature-type design from original *Theros* 2013). Constellation renders this *literal* at the rules-text level: every enchantment entering is a Nyx-event, every Constellation trigger is the mortal-plane reacting to that Nyx-event. The mechanic's narrative read is the gods themselves noticing — the constellation lights up, and the mortal plane's enchantment-coded creatures and spells respond.

Eutropia the Twice-Favored (THB-216, in corpus) is one of the THB non-enchantment Constellation creatures the design-knob solution enabled — the very creature-class extension Rosewater names in the article as the Play Design fix. The article's specific previewed Constellation card is **Archon of Sun's Grace** (THB-3, not yet in corpus), the enchantment-creature payoff the article uses to demonstrate the returning mechanic's flavor at the Heliod-cult register. Eutropia is structurally the design-knob's mechanical beneficiary; Archon is the article's narrative demonstration card. Both points hold; they are distinct.
fixes.rationale: Replaces the line-37 over-claim ('Eutropia is canonically the article's preview-example non-enchantment Constellation creature — the article previews her precisely to demonstrate the design-knob solution') with WebFetch-verified language. Eutropia retains her design-knob-beneficiary role at the rules-text-mechanical scale (she IS a non-enchantment THB Constellation creature, which IS the article-explained design knob); Archon of Sun's Grace is correctly identified as the article's actual previewed card. Surfaces the distinction between 'design knob beneficiary' (the class of cards the article describes) and 'previewed example' (the specific card the article highlights). Both are useful framings; the prior text conflated them.

fixes.before: - **Eutropia the Twice-Favored** (THB-216, uncommon green-blue, Sara Winters art, **trivia-passed with explicit Rosewater article citation**) — `{1}{G}{U}` Legendary Creature — Human Wizard 2/2 (note: a *creature*, NOT an Enchantment Creature, per the article-explained Play Design knob). Oracle text: *"Constellation — Whenever an enchantment you control enters, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature. That creature gains flying until end of turn."* The mechanic's *headline-Constellation-on-non-enchantment rendering* — the canonical article-previewed example of placing Constellation on a non-enchantment creature to break the original 2014 feedback-loop concern. Per Eutropia's in-corpus trivia citation, Rosewater's *"At Death's Door, Part 2"* (2020-01-06) explicitly previews her as the demonstration card for the design-knob solution. Eutropia is also the canonical *Twice-Favored* — devoted to both Nylea (green, wind / hunt) and Thassa (blue, sea / tides), a Theros lore rarity, with art-direction-coordinated wind-and-tide swirling magic and dual-bowl offering iconography.
fixes.after: - **Eutropia the Twice-Favored** (THB-216, uncommon green-blue, Sara Winters art, **trivia-passed with explicit Rosewater article citation**) — `{1}{G}{U}` Legendary Creature — Human Wizard 2/2 (note: a *creature*, NOT an Enchantment Creature, per the article-explained Play Design knob). Oracle text: *"Constellation — Whenever an enchantment you control enters, put a +1/+1 counter on target creature. That creature gains flying until end of turn."* The mechanic's *headline-Constellation-on-non-enchantment rendering* — Eutropia is structurally one of the THB cards demonstrating the Play Design knob (extending Constellation to non-enchantment creatures to break the original 2014 feedback-loop concern). Per Rosewater's *"At Death's Door, Part 2"* (2020-01-06): *"if we wanted to do constellation, we'd have to be willing to put it on some nonenchantment cards."* The article's specific previewed Constellation card is Archon of Sun's Grace (THB-3), an Enchantment Creature payoff at the Heliod-cult register; Eutropia is the article-explained design-knob's mechanical beneficiary in the BBL corpus. Eutropia is also the canonical *Twice-Favored* — devoted to both Nylea (green, wind / hunt) and Thassa (blue, sea / tides), a Theros lore rarity, with art-direction-coordinated wind-and-tide swirling magic and dual-bowl offering iconography.
fixes.rationale: Replaces the line-50 over-claim ('explicitly previews her as the demonstration card for the design-knob solution') with WebFetch-verified language. Quotes Rosewater verbatim from the WebFetch return. Correctly attributes the article's specific preview card to Archon of Sun's Grace. Eutropia's design-knob-beneficiary role retained — the entire 'headline-Constellation-on-non-enchantment rendering' framing is intact, only the false 'article previewed Eutropia specifically' claim is corrected.

fixes.before: - **The original 2014 Constellation was templated on enchantments ONLY; the THB 2020 return extends to non-enchantments.** Per Rosewater's *"At Death's Door, Part 2"* (2020-01-06), the Play Design team identified the enchantments-only templating as the source of the original mechanic's balance-feedback-loop concern. The THB return's signature design knob is the extension to non-enchantment creatures (Eutropia the Twice-Favored is the article's preview example) and to non-enchantment instants / sorceries (no in-corpus example currently). All four in-corpus anchors are creatures; three of them (Captivating Unicorn, Sage of Mysteries, Setessan Skirmisher) are Enchantment Creatures and one (Eutropia) is a non-enchantment Legendary Creature — Eutropia is the design-knob exemplar in the BBL corpus.
fixes.after: - **The original 2014 Constellation was templated on enchantments ONLY; the THB 2020 return extends to non-enchantments.** Per Rosewater's *"At Death's Door, Part 2"* (2020-01-06), the Play Design team identified the enchantments-only templating as the source of the original mechanic's balance-feedback-loop concern; Rosewater quotes Sam Stoddard (initial set design lead): *"if we wanted to do constellation, we'd have to be willing to put it on some nonenchantment cards."* The THB return's signature design knob is the extension to non-enchantment creatures and to non-enchantment instants / sorceries. The article's specific preview card is Archon of Sun's Grace (THB-3, not in corpus) — an Enchantment Creature payoff at the Heliod-cult register. All four in-corpus anchors are creatures; three of them (Captivating Unicorn, Sage of Mysteries, Setessan Skirmisher) are Enchantment Creatures and one (Eutropia the Twice-Favored) is a non-enchantment Legendary Creature — Eutropia is the corpus's instance of the design-knob class (Constellation-on-non-enchantment-creature), the specific card-class the article makes the case for.
fixes.rationale: Replaces the line-72 over-claim ('Eutropia the Twice-Favored is the article's preview example') with WebFetch-verified language. Adds the Stoddard quote (verbatim from WebFetch) as the article's actual design-knob articulation. Eutropia retains her design-knob-exemplar-class role; Archon of Sun's Grace correctly identified as the article's specific preview card.

verification_command: cd 'C:\Users\alexa\Desktop\Bulk Graph Bundler' && grep -c 'preview-example\|article previews her\|preview example.*Eutropia\|previews her as the demonstration' cards/_symbols/constellation.md && grep -c 'Archon of Sun' cards/_symbols/constellation.md
uncertainty_notes: (1) Wave-207 sidecar at reports/edges_pending/wave207-constellation-symbol-node-commission.json line 34 carries the same confab in its description field. The sidecar is a historical record of the wave-as-executed; correcting it would rewrite history. Standard practice per the wave-200-onward 'sidecars are receipts' discipline is to leave the sidecar alone and let the corrected node body be the corpus's current truth. The operator may choose to add a footnote in the sidecar's discipline_notes if a future provenance pass wants to mark the wave-207 confab as known-and-corrected. (2) The Eutropia card trivia at cards/magic-the-gathering/theros-beyond-death/216-eutropia-the-twice-favored.md line 93 is already hedged carefully ('Cards like Eutropia were described as the key knob' + 'Rosewater even previewed a non-enchantment Constellation card specifically in that article' — the latter is true if read as 'Archon of Sun's Grace is non-enchantment-Constellation', which it is since Archon is an Enchantment Creature, NOT a non-enchantment creature). Re-reading the trivia line: this is actually subtly wrong too — the article's preview card is Archon of Sun's Grace which IS an Enchantment Creature, not a non-enchantment Constellation card. The trivia bullet conflates 'Constellation on creatures' with 'Constellation on non-enchantment cards'. Recommend a separate spot-edit on the trivia bullet to disambiguate, OR accept that the trivia's looser framing 'Cards like Eutropia' makes it defensible as 'Eutropia is one of the cards that benefit from the design-knob class extension'. Curator judgment call; sidecar fixes only target the node body. (3) Archon of Sun's Grace (THB-3) confirmed via Scryfall as Enchantment Creature — Pegasus, NOT a non-enchantment creature. The article previews an Enchantment Creature; the design-knob it explains is 'put Constellation on non-enchantment cards too'. The article does both — preview Archon as a Constellation card AND explain that the broader knob enables Constellation on non-enchantments (Eutropia among them). The fixed prose now correctly captures both.
